mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate indicates a tight labor market, boosting corporate profits and driving S&P 500 returns over 20 days. The signal activates when unemployment falls below 4%.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 4%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with stable inflation (low CPI) signals a healthy economy, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days. The effect requires both indicators to move in tandem.
failure_condition: The joint movement of GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns, evidenced by a 3-year rolling Rank IC < 0.1 or directional disagreement in 50%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising interest rates and high inflation pressure S&P 500 returns negatively. The signal activates when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 300.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 300, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 returns over 20 days. The signal is active when INDPRO increases by 1%+ month-over-month.
failure_condition: SPX returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO changes over 3-year rolling windows, or the signal fails in 50%+ of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth reflects robust labor market conditions, increasing consumer spending and driving S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days. The effect is strongest when PAYEMS exceeds 150k new jobs.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS > 150k, or the 5d return correlation with PAYEMS drops below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal increased construction activity and consumer confidence, boosting S&P 500 returns over 20 days. The effect is strongest when HOUST exceeds 1.45 million units.
failure_condition: SPX returns show no significant link to HOUST levels over 3-year rolling windows, or the signal fails in 50%+ of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) signals shifting consumer demand patterns, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days. The effect requires both indicators to move in opposite directions.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict SPX returns, evidenced by a 3-year rolling Rank IC < 0.1 or directional disagreement in 50%+ of cases.
